# Uni-CLI

Uni-CLI is the universal command surface for AI agents. It exposes websites,
desktop apps, local tools, external CLIs, and agent backends through searchable
commands and a shared v2 AgentEnvelope output contract.

## Start Here

- Overview: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/
- Getting started: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/guide/getting-started
- Sites catalog: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/reference/sites
- Agent integrations: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/guide/integrations
- Adapter authoring: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/guide/adapters
- Self-repair loop: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/guide/self-repair

## Reference

- Adapter format: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/ADAPTER-FORMAT
- Pipeline steps: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/reference/pipeline
- Exit codes: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/reference/exit-codes
- Maintenance tools: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/reference/maintenance
- Release process: https://olo-dot-io.github.io/Uni-CLI/reference/release

## Command Shape

```bash
npm install -g @zenalexa/unicli
unicli search "your intent"
unicli <site> <command> [args] [-f json|md|yaml|csv|compact]
```

Use `unicli search` before guessing a site command. Use `-f json` for scripts
and machine-oriented consumers. If a command fails, inspect the structured
error envelope, edit the adapter path, and run `unicli repair <site> <command>`.
